hero.title: Encode intention. Decode software.
hero.subtitle: 46 providers. 3,600+ models. One structured workflow that plans before it builds, audits before it ships, and verifies before it reports done.

agents.label: Subagent System
agents.title: Five role agents. Parallel. Adversarial. De-anchored.
agents.desc: Spawn executors for implementation, architects for review, critics for plan quality. They run in parallel, don't duplicate each other's work, and when corrected, generate 3+ fresh hypotheses instead of patching their prior answer.

pipeline.label: Workflow
pipeline.title: IPABCD — six gates from idea to verified delivery
pipeline.desc: No guessing. No skipping. Each stage has explicit gates. The agent cannot report done without passing adversarial review.

providers.label: All Providers
providers.title: 46 providers. Switch models mid-conversation.
providers.desc: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, Groq, DeepSeek, Mistral, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex, OpenRouter, Ollama, and 35 more. API keys, OAuth, or local inference. 1M context window supported for Claude models.

speed.label: Performance
speed.title: Bun-native. Fast startup. Streaming-first.
speed.desc: Built on Bun from the ground up. Prompt caching, streaming tool output, background job management, and compaction keep long sessions fast. No webpack, no transpile step, no cold-start lag.

tools.label: Built-in
tools.title: 40+ Tools
tools.desc: From AST-aware code search to browser automation. Essential tools load by default; discoverable tools activate on demand.

workflow.title: IPABCD Workflow
workflow.lead: Six stages from vague idea to verified delivery. Each stage has explicit gates — no guessing, no skipping.

i.title: Interview
i.purpose: Clarify ambiguous requirements via Socratic interview with mathematical ambiguity scoring.
i.input: Vague user request or feature idea.
i.gate: Ambiguity score below threshold — dual-audit confirmation.
i.detail: The interview stage uses a Socratic questioning loop to surface hidden assumptions, resolve ambiguities, and produce a concrete specification.

p.title: Plan
p.purpose: Consensus planning with critic revision loop.
p.input: Spec from Interview, or a clear requirement.
p.detail: The planning stage writes a concrete implementation plan. A Critic subagent reviews through multiple rounds; the plan advances only when the Critic responds with OKAY.

a.title: Audit
a.purpose: Independent feasibility check by Planner and Architect subagents.
a.gate: Both audit_status = pass required for A to B.
a.detail: Two independent subagents audit the plan without seeing each other's output. Both must PASS.

b.title: Build
b.purpose: Direct implementation of the audited plan.
b.gate: verification_status = done required for B to C.
b.detail: The main session implements the plan. Bounded slices may be delegated to executor subagents. A read-only adversarial verifier checks the implementation.

c.title: Check
c.output: All gates pass + adversarial review clear.
c.gate: 3-way reject: code to B, plan to P, spec to I.
c.detail: Runs bun run check + affected tests, then an adversarial reviewer inspects for contract violations and regressions.

d.title: Done
d.detail: Closes the cycle with structured summary, decisions, and verification evidence.

control.title: Human Control Model
control.intro: Two control modes depending on agent autonomy.

hitl.label: Human-in-the-Loop
hitl.desc: User advances each stage manually. Maximum oversight.

hotl.label: Human-on-the-Loop
hotl.desc: Goal wraps PABCD. Agent advances autonomously, pausing at approval gates.

control.note: Both modes enforce the same gates. HOTL automates transitions while preserving verification checkpoints.
